heading: Permissions Required
body:
**S3:**S3You must have:* A federated database instance configured for an S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket with read and write
  permissions or [s3:PutObject](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/dev/using-with-s3-actions.html#using-with-s3-actions-related-to-objects)
  permissions.

* A MongoDB user with the [atlasAdmin](atlas/security-add-mongodb-users)
  role or a custom role with the [OUT\_TO\_S3](outToS3) privilege.**Azure:**AzureYou must have:* A federated database instance configured for Azure (Microsoft Azure) Blob Storage with an [Azure Role](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/blobs/assign-azure-role-data-access?tabs=portal)
  that has read and write permissions.

* A MongoDB user with the [atlasAdmin](atlas/security-add-mongodb-users)
  role or a custom role with the [OUT\_TO\_AZURE](outToAzure)
  privilege.**Gcp:**GCPYou must have:* A federated database instance configured for a Google Cloud Storage bucket with access to a [GCP
  Service Account](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/manage-access-service-accounts).

* A MongoDB user with the [atlasAdmin](atlas/security-add-mongodb-users)
  role or a custom role with the [OUT\_TO\_GCP](outToGCP)
  privilege.**Atlas:**Atlas Cluster> **Note:** 
>
> To use [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out) to write to
> a collection in a different database on the same Atlas cluster,
> your Atlas cluster must be on MongoDB version 7.0 or later.You must be a database user with one of the following roles:* [readWriteAnyDatabase](manual/reference/built-in-roles)

* [atlasAdmin](manual/reference/built-in-roles)

* A custom role with the following privileges:

  - [insert](manual/reference/privilege-actions) and

  - [remove](manual/reference/privilege-actions)
